Tests the behavior of the `Function.prototype.call` method in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates the use of the `Function.prototype.call` method in JavaScript to invoke a function with a specified `this` value and arguments. The function [`f`](<#prototypef>) is defined to accept three parameters: `arg1`, `arg2`, and `arg3`, which it assigns to properties `string`, `number`, and `boolean` on the `this` object. The `this_arg` object is used as the `this` context when calling [`f`](<#prototypef>) with the `call` method, passing the arguments `'s'`, `1`, and `true`. The code then uses assertions to verify that the properties of `this_arg` have been correctly set to the expected values. This script provides a narrow functionality focused on illustrating the behavior of the `call` method in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### this\_arg
- **Type**: ``object``
- **Description**: An empty object that is used as the `this` context for the function `f` when it is called using `Function.prototype.call`. The properties of this object are set by the function `f` based on the arguments passed to it.
- **Use**: Used as the `this` context for the function `f` to dynamically assign properties to it.


# Functions

---
### f<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/function.prototype.f}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/function.prototype.js#L15>)

Assigns the provided arguments to properties of the `this` context.
- **Inputs**:
    - `arg1`: The value to assign to the `string` property of the `this` context.
    - `arg2`: The value to assign to the `number` property of the `this` context.
    - `arg3`: The value to assign to the `boolean` property of the `this` context.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Assign `arg1` to `this.string`.
    - Assign `arg2` to `this.number`.
    - Assign `arg3` to `this.boolean`.
- **Output**: No return value; modifies the `this` context by setting its properties.
